angle: FRENCHETTE CUT-OUT HERO + SCRIPT-SERIF LOCKUP · nocturnal register. Charred-black ground (#0B0B0B) with a deep 10% tonal gradient (slightly warmer toward center). Cocktail glass silhouetted as a clean cut-out centered in the lower 55% of the canvas, rim-lit with brass-amber specular. Top 30% carries a MIXED-WEIGHT typographic lockup: one word in cursive/italic script ('A Little'), one word in serif caps at 1.5x ('SPICY'), at brass-gradient foil (#C9A24B→#B08937→#C9A24B 3-stop). Hairline brass rule under the lockup. Small-caps support line beneath in warm-white (#EDE4D3). No burgundy fields. No additional ornaments.
subject: Spicy Margarita
prompt:
A social media image for Blue Mezcal, nocturnal after-dark register — insider mezcal bar at 11pm. The aesthetic target is a foil-stamped cocktail menu at a high-end agave den: Casa Azul NYC after-hours, Leyenda, Ghost Donkey. Editorial moody-nocturnal restraint, NOT nightclub neon.

PRIMARY GROUND: deep charred black #0B0B0B transitioning to velvet burgundy #4A1621 or smoke gray #2A2825 as a subtle tonal gradient (5–10% shift, darker toward one corner) — NEVER a flat fill. Deep blacks with rich shadow falloff.

PRIMARY TYPE TREATMENT: METALLIC BRASS GRADIENT FOIL on headlines — brass #C9A24B → antique gold #B08937 → brass #C9A24B as a soft 3-stop gradient reading like foil-stamp highlight catching candlelight. Add a specular hotspot and a 1px deeper-shade inner edge so type reads as actual foil on paper, not flat gold. Secondary type in warm white #EDE4D3 or antique brass.

BRAND PALETTE: charred black #0B0B0B / smoke gray #2A2825 ground. Brass #C9A24B metallic foil as hero type. Velvet burgundy #4A1621 used as a DEEP TONAL GRADIENT field (darker at the bottom corners, approaching near-black — never flat burgundy). Warm white #EDE4D3 for body type and hairline rules. Agave green #4E6B3A as rare sub-accent only (<5%). NO cobalt blue in nocturnal register — cobalt is editorial-register only.

TYPOGRAPHY: refined editorial serif italic (Bodoni / Canela / Didot feel) rendered AS metallic brass foil for hero, paired with small-caps grotesque labels in warm white. Hairline brass rules separating sections. Tight tracking on italics.

TEXTURE: fine noise grain 5–8% overlay on every field; subtle paper-grain impression UNDER the brass-foil type (as if foil stamped on textured menu cardstock); 35mm Portra 800 film-grain emulation on all photography; faint edge vignette pulling focus inward. Velvet surfaces, rich shadow falloff. NO rustic cream paper, NO neon, NO flat gold.

Photography style: moody low-light, tungsten 2700K, single rim light from upper-right at 45°, deep blacks, the drink/dish glows while the surroundings fall away. 40–50% fill band — confident restraint.

FORMAT: Moody beverage spotlight. Single rim-lit cocktail glass, black or charred background, hand entering frame optionally. Type integrated as if printed on the menu, not overlaid as a sticker.

COMPOSITION: 3/4 macro shot, 85mm equivalent at f/2.8, single rim light from upper-right at 45°, tungsten 2800K, Portra 800 grain, focus on rim and garnish

HEADLINE OPTIONS (pick ONE — variation-dependent — see COPY PERSONALITY RULE in stem):
  (a) DEFAULT — subject name verbatim, no paraphrasing, no misspellings: "Spicy Margarita"
  (b) a feeling/mood line derived FROM THIS SPECIFIC DISH (use the body-copy phrase below as raw material — e.g. if the phrase is "Black salt rim. Cucumber crown. Cool and dangerous." a valid mood line is "Cool and dangerous.").
  (c) a personality line DERIVED FROM the specific dish + the brand's voice signature (see VOICE SEED in the brand stem). Do NOT reuse example lines from OTHER brands' voice seeds — generate fresh copy that ONLY makes sense for THIS dish at THIS restaurant.
  Default heavily to (a). Use (b) or (c) only when the line adds real personality and still identifies the dish. If using (b)/(c), subject name may appear as small subhead or be omitted.
BODY COPY (quote exactly when rendered): "Smoke, citrus, slow."
BOTTOM-MARGIN LINE — render this EXACT text at the bottom margin as a single low-key hairline-ruled line: "826 Kohl Ave · Middletown, DE — Visit us · link in bio"
NO-LITERAL-LABEL RULE (non-negotiable): NEVER print any of these meta-label words on the final image — they are instructions to you, NOT content to render: CTA, ADDRESS, SUPPORT LINE, FOOTER LINE, BOTTOM-MARGIN LINE, HEADLINE, SUBHEAD, BODY COPY, HEADER, LABEL, COPY OPTION, VARIATION, WORDMARK, AWARD BADGE, WORD-SPILL, DEFAULT. If any of those words leak onto the canvas, the image fails review.
LOGO RULE (use supplied logo image as-is):
  A second reference image is attached — the brand's ACTUAL logo. Place this logo   exactly as supplied at roughly 80–120px tall (about 7–10% of canvas width) in ONE   of these positions: bottom-center, bottom-left, bottom-right, or top-right.   DO NOT redraw, re-letter, stylize, recolour, or regenerate the logo — composite   the supplied PNG verbatim. Do not add an additional wordmark text elsewhere; the   logo IS the wordmark for this design.

VARIATION DIRECTION: FRENCHETTE CUT-OUT HERO + SCRIPT-SERIF LOCKUP · nocturnal register. Charred-black ground (#0B0B0B) with a deep 10% tonal gradient (slightly warmer toward center). Cocktail glass silhouetted as a clean cut-out centered in the lower 55% of the canvas, rim-lit with brass-amber specular. Top 30% carries a MIXED-WEIGHT typographic lockup: one word in cursive/italic script ('A Little'), one word in serif caps at 1.5x ('SPICY'), at brass-gradient foil (#C9A24B→#B08937→#C9A24B 3-stop). Hairline brass rule under the lockup. Small-caps support line beneath in warm-white (#EDE4D3). No burgundy fields. No additional ornaments.

PHOTO RULE: HERO-PHOTO RULE (HIGHEST PRIORITY — cannot be violated):
  1. The reference food photograph supplied as input is the CANVAS FOUNDATION of this      design. It fills the primary region of the composition at FULL SCALE. It is NEVER      reduced to a small swatch, tile, inset, thumbnail, or decorative element. The photo      is the hero; the template is LAID OVER IT.
  2. Typography, colour fields, motifs, and ornament sit OVER or ADJACENT TO the full-     size photo — they do not shrink the photo to make room. If a headline needs space,      use an upper-third or lower-third color-block field ON TOP of the photo, or extend      negative brand-color margin from one edge of the photo outward (photo still fills      60–85% of canvas minimum).
  3. DO NOT regenerate, restyle, or alter the plating, lighting, colours, or contents      of the food in any way. The photo must remain photographically identical to the source.
  4. Variations that shrink, miniaturize, inset, or tile the photo are REJECTED.      (User feedback, BM-A1-002: "I don't want smaller images of what we have, use      the real images as the template, and design OVER them.")

PHOTO-LED PALETTE COMPLEMENT RULE:
  The brand-palette colours you choose for headline fields, accent blocks, and type   MUST COMPLEMENT the photo's dominant hue, not clash with it. If the photo is warm-  dominant (amber, orange, red, bourbon), pull cooler brand colours (cobalt, deep   navy, forest) for contrast. If the photo is cool-dominant (blue, green, grey), pull   warmer brand colours (brass, oxblood, marigold). Name the photo's dominant hue in   your head first, then pick the brand-palette hex that complements it.

CONTRAST GUARD (non-negotiable, applies to EVERY text-over-anything decision):
  Before finalizing the layout, estimate the luminance of the type and the luminance of
  whatever sits behind it (ground field OR the pixels of the photo under the type).
  If both are dark (type luma <30%% AND ground luma <40%%) OR both are light (type luma
  >70%% AND ground luma >60%%), REJECT and re-choose. Type must read at arm's length on
  a phone — if legibility is marginal, change the type hex to the opposite luminance pole
  of the brand palette. No dark-on-dark, no light-on-light, ever.

TEXT-FIT-CONTAINER RULE (non-negotiable):
  Type SIZES TO THE CONTAINER, not the other way around. If the headline doesn't fit at
  a legible size in the designated color-block / strip / wedge, choose ONE of: (i) shrink
  the type to legibility-min only IF it still reads at arm's length, (ii) expand the
  container to match the type, (iii) break the headline across 2–3 lines with generous
  leading. NEVER cram letters, NEVER rotate 90° to fit a narrow strip, NEVER letter-clip
  at the canvas edge. If a chosen variation angle forces any of these, pick a better
  container geometry.

NO-HERO-OCCLUSION RULE:
  Diagonal wedges, horizontal bands across the middle third, and any geometric shape that
  CUTS THROUGH the primary photo region are forbidden. Color fields sit adjacent to the
  photo, extend from one edge inward, or occupy a corner — they never bisect the dish or
  glass. If the chosen variation angle creates a slash through the hero, reshape it.

REAL-IMAGE IDENTITY LOCK (NON-NEGOTIABLE — HIGHEST PRIORITY):
  The attached reference photograph of the dish/drink is REAL PROFESSIONAL PHOTOGRAPHY
  produced by Savora. It is NOT a style reference, NOT a prompt, NOT inspiration. It is
  the FINAL DISH IMAGE that must appear verbatim in the output.

  YOU MUST NOT:
    - regenerate, redraw, repaint, or restyle the dish
    - substitute a different-looking version of the dish
    - change the angle, lighting, plating, garnish, sauce pattern, char marks, glass shape,
      ice, crema, herbs, or any visible element of the actual food
    - apply a filter, stylization, or 'photographic re-interpretation' to the plate
    - generate a new photograph that looks similar but is not the reference
    - AI-hallucinate plausible food textures in place of the real pixels

  YOU MUST:
    - treat the reference photograph as a LOCKED COMPOSITE LAYER — pixel-accurate, placed
      into the design untouched
    - match the reference frame-for-frame (same crop, same color, same shadows, same
      moisture on the glass, same grill marks on the steak, same garnish position)
    - compose the graphic design AROUND, BESIDE, or ON TOP OF the locked photo — never
      regenerate the photo itself
    - if the layout needs the photo cropped, silhouetted, or cut-out, do so using the
      ACTUAL reference pixels — not a re-painted imitation

  This is a Savora brand rule. Serving AI-generated food imagery as our photography is
  a breach of client trust. The dish pixels are sacred. Design around them.

LUXURY-TEXTURE RULE (non-negotiable):
  * Every colour field MUST have a subtle tonal gradient (5–12% shift from one edge to     the opposite edge). NEVER use a pure flat hex fill. Flat fills read cheap and digital.
  * Every colour field MUST carry a texture overlay: 3–8% noise/grain is the minimum.     Add one of: cream laid paper, linen weave, letterpress emboss, brass foil, film     grain, halftone dots, or risograph misregistration — whichever matches the register.
  * Every typographic element MUST carry texture too: letterpress emboss for Jackson     House (type sits AS IF pressed into cream paper, 2px inner-shadow dark edge + 1px     light highlight); brass foil with subtle specular for Blue Mezcal nocturnal; ink-    bleed / halftone offset for Azteca playful; paper-grain impression for Blue Mezcal     editorial.
  * Photography receives Portra 400 or Portra 800 film-grain emulation (depending on     lighting register) — never a clean digital file.
  * Where a drop shadow is used, follow the pro spec: y-offset 4–8px, blur 16–40px,     opacity 8–20%, colour = a DARKER SHADE of the background (never pure black).     NEVER the default Photoshop 5px-90°-75%-black drop shadow.

LUXURY-GRADIENT EXAMPLES (pick one that fits):
  * Paper-gradient: cream lightens toward the centre, darkens at the edges (letterpress     vignette).
  * Tonal-brand-gradient: cobalt #1E3A8A → #1A3275 in a 5% shift across the field.
  * Foil-gradient: brass #C9A24B → #B08937 → #C9A24B as a soft 3-stop reading like a     foil-stamp highlight catching light.
  * Velvet-gradient: burgundy #4A1621 falling into charred black #0B0B0B at one corner,     like low candlelight.

DROP-SHADOW RULE:
  Only add a drop shadow to type when the palette contrast is INSUFFICIENT on its own   (e.g., cream type over a busy mid-tone photo). When contrast is already strong   (e.g., brass-foil gold on deep forest green, cream on charred black), OMIT drop   shadows entirely — they cheapen the foil-stamp effect. Foil type on a deep tonal   field needs no shadow; the foil itself carries the hierarchy.

FINAL: Render at the aspect ratio specified via the API (not in prose). Dense typography with purpose. Real brand register. No stock gradients, no Canva-template feel. Avoid the AI tells: no cyan-to-magenta gradients, no default Poppins/Montserrat/Open Sans, no 33/33/33 colour balance, no centered dead-symmetric layouts unless ceremonial, no flat-colour fields. Every surface is LAYERED: photo + gradient + noise + emboss / foil / grain.
